## Fire Response per API 2021 and NFPA 30

### Initial Assessment
Middle tank fire threatens four neighbors. 25 mph winds with 35 mph gusts - EXTREME FIRE SPREAD RISK. High temperature (98°F) accelerates fire. 600 barrels condensate burning with high vapor pressure.

### Primary Strategy per NFPA 30
1. IMMEDIATE cooling of ALL exposed tanks with water deluge
2. APPLY foam to burning tank
3. Pump out neighboring tanks if safe to do so
4. Establish water supply for sustained operations

### Cooling Water Requirements per NFPA 30
Exposed tank cooling rate: 0.25 gpm/sq ft
Tank surface area (cylinder + roof): ~3,200 sq ft
Four exposed tanks: 12,800 sq ft
Total cooling water: 3,200 gpm

### Foam Application per NFPA 11
Application rate: 0.16 gpm/sq ft
Tank surface: 2,800 sq ft
Foam solution required: 450 gpm
Foam duration: minimum 30 minutes
Foam concentrate: 3% = 13.5 gpm concentrate

### Evacuation Zones
Immediate facility: Full evacuation
1,500-foot radius around tanks (wind-driven fire risk)
Residential area 2,000 feet north: Shelter in place, prepare for evacuation

### Pump Out Decision
YES - pump out neighboring tanks if:
- Personnel can approach safely
- Transfer pumps are operational
- Receiving capacity available
Reduces fuel load if fire spreads

### References: API 2021, NFPA 30, NFPA 11, NFPA 780

abnormal_variants.description: Boil-over occurs during firefighting
abnormal_variants.modified_expected_response: BOIL-OVER EMERGENCY - TOTAL EVACUATION. Condensate boil-over creates explosive vapor cloud. Evacuate all personnel to 3,000 feet minimum. Defensive operations only. Do not approach tanks. Notify all surrounding facilities.

abnormal_variants.description: Second tank ignites from radiant heat
abnormal_variants.modified_expected_response: MULTIPLE TANK FIRE - ESCALATE RESPONSE. Second tank fire indicates inadequate cooling. Increase water application. Request additional fire departments. Consider foam blanket on exposed tanks. Evacuation radius increase to 2,000 feet.

name: Sour Gas Release Response - H2S Emergency
situation: Leak detected on sour gas gathering pipeline. Pipeline typically carries gas with 800 ppm H2S. Leak is at flange connection on 8-inch pipeline operating at 1,200 psig. Field personnel with H2S monitors detected 25 ppm H2S at 75 feet downwind. Wind is 10 mph from northeast. Temperature 92°F. Nearest residence is 1,500 feet southwest. Wellpad with 8 producing wells is 1,200 feet northeast (upwind). Facility has H2S contingency procedures per 192.615. Two workers on site, both H2S trained with monitors.
task: Execute H2S emergency response per NACE MR0175 and 192.615. Determine evacuation zones based on H2S concentration and dispersion modeling, specify PPE requirements for different zones, establish monitoring protocols, determine if wellpad should be shut in, and calculate potential H2S concentrations at various distances.
expected_response_detailed:
## Sour Gas Response per 192.615 and NACE MR0175

### H2S Hazard Assessment
Pipeline: 800 ppm H2S source concentration
Detected: 25 ppm at 75 feet downwind
ERPG-2 (Emergency Response Planning Guideline): 30 ppm
IDLH (Immediately Dangerous to Life/Health): 100 ppm
10 ppm TWA (8-hour permissible exposure)

### Dispersion Analysis
Current: 25 ppm at 75 feet
ERPG-2 (30 ppm) estimated at ~100 feet
10 ppm contour estimated at ~400 feet
1 ppm contour estimated at ~1,200 feet

### Evacuation Zones
RED Zone (IDLH >100 ppm): Estimated 50-foot radius
YELLOW Zone (ERPG-2 10-30 ppm): 100-400 feet
GREEN Zone (<10 ppm): Beyond 400 feet

Residents at 1,500 feet southwest: Below 10 ppm, shelter in place

### PPE Requirements
Current 25 ppm: Half-mask respirator acceptable for brief entry
>50 ppm: Full-face respirator required
>100 ppm: SCBA (Self-Contained Breathing Apparatus) mandatory
Leak repair: SCBA required regardless of concentration

### Wellpad Decision
Current 25 ppm, wind away from wellpad
Wellpad 1,200 feet upwind: CONTINUE operation
If wind shifts southwest or H2S increases above 50 ppm: Shut-in wells

### References: 49 CFR 192.615, NACE MR0175, API RP 55, NIOSH REL

abnormal_variants.description: Wind shifts bringing H2S cloud toward wellpad with 12 workers
abnormal_variants.modified_expected_response: WIND SHIFT - IMMEDIATE EVACUATION. H2S cloud now threatening wellpad with 12 workers. Immediate wellpad evacuation required. All personnel SCBA or evacuated. Shut in all wells immediately. Notify wellpad with emergency radio.

abnormal_variants.description: H2S monitor alarms at 75 ppm during approach
abnormal_variants.modified_expected_response: HIGH H2S - SCBA MANDATORY. 75 ppm exceeds safe working limits. All personnel must don SCBA immediately. Evacuate non-essential personnel. Only essential personnel with SCBA may approach. Establish 200-foot exclusion.

name: Valve Operations Under Pressure - Large Bore MOV
situation: 36-inch motor operated valve (MOV) on transmission pipeline requires maintenance on motor actuator. Valve currently 100% open. Valve class is API 6D soft-seated ball valve rated for 1,440 psig. Current pipeline pressure 1,250 psig. Actuator must be removed for rebuild, which requires cycling valve to mid-position. Valve not operated in 3 years. Valve located in remote area with limited access. No bypass available. Temperature 35°F. Valve has position indicators but no partial stroke testing capability.
task: Determine if valve can be safely operated under current pressure conditions per API 6D and API RP 553. Identify risks of valve seizure and specify precautions. Determine if pressure reduction required before operation. Address cold temperature considerations.
expected_response_detailed:
## Valve Operation per API 6D and API RP 553

### Valve Condition Assessment
Soft-seated ball valve - API 6D rated for 1,440 psig
Not operated in 3 years - EXTENDED PERIOD
Current pressure: 1,250 psig (87% of rating)
Temperature: 35°F - COLD WEATHER

### API 6D Requirements
Ball valves per API 6D Section 10:
- Valves shall open/close at rated differential pressure
- Soft seats rated for frequent operation
- Design allows operation at full differential

### Risk Assessment
Valve Seizure Risk: HIGH after 3 years without operation
Factors increasing risk:
- Soft seats may adhere to ball
- Cold temperature (35°F) increases seal adhesion
- High differential pressure (1,250 psid)
- No partial stroke history

### Precautions Required
1. Attempt manual override first if available
2. Slow opening/closing (minimum 30 seconds)
3. Monitor torque during operation
4. Have contingency plan if valve sticks
5. Warm valve area if possible before operation

### Pressure Operation Decision
OK to operate at 1,250 psig per API 6D
No pressure reduction required per code
However, given 3-year inactivity, consider reducing to 1,000 psig if operationally feasible

### Cold Temperature Considerations
API 6D valves rated to -20°F
35°F is within rating
But cold increases seal adhesion risk
Consider pre-heating valve body to 50°F if possible

### References: API 6D Section 10, API RP 553
expected_elements: API 6D rated for full pressure operation

abnormal_variants.description: Valve will not cycle - appears seized at 100% open
abnormal_variants.modified_expected_response: SEIZED VALVE - DO NOT FORCE. Stop operation immediately. If seized: do not exceed rated torque, apply incremental pressure cycling, consider hot tapping bypass, may require line shutdown. Forcing causes catastrophic failure. Document for replacement planning.

abnormal_variants.description: Valve cycles but excessive torque required
abnormal_variants.modified_expected_response: HIGH TORQUE - DEGRADED CONDITION. Excessive torque indicates seat damage or debris. Complete operation to mid-position but do not return to service without inspection. Plan for valve replacement. Document torque values for trending.

name: Abnormal Operations - Compressor Surge Event
situation: Centrifugal compressor operating at design conditions (1,100 psig suction, 1,500 psig discharge, 350 MMSCFD flow) experiences sudden surge event. Flow reverses momentarily, vibration increases to 0.9 in/s, temperature spikes 50°F. Surge occurred when downstream block valve inadvertently closed by operator error. Control system detected surge and opened recycle valve after 3-second delay. Unit is currently in recycle mode, but throughput severely reduced. Station operator reports unusual noise and vibration from compressor. Weather is calm, no other alarms present. Unit has 12,000 hours since last overhaul.
task: Respond to abnormal operation per 49 CFR 192.615. Assess damage potential from surge event, determine appropriate actions to return to normal operation, specify inspection requirements before returning to full load, address control system response adequacy, and document the incident.
expected_response_detailed:
## Abnormal Operation Response per 192.615

### Surge Assessment
Flow reversal, high vibration (0.9 in/s), temperature spike all indicate severe surge.
Surge causes:
- Seal damage (dry gas seals vulnerable)
- Impeller damage (aerodynamic loading reversal)
- Thrust bearing damage (axial load reversal)
- Increased clearances

### Immediate Actions
1. MAINTAIN recycle valve open - prevents repeat surge
2. DO NOT restart to full load until inspection complete
3. Investigate valve closure - operator retraining required
4. Assess vibration - 0.9 in/s exceeds alarm (0.5 in/s)

### Control System Response Evaluation
3-second delay to recycle opening:
- API 618 recommends <1 second
- 3 seconds is MARGINAL - may have caused damage
- Review anti-surge control settings
- Consider reducing surge margin

### Inspection Requirements before Return
Mandatory inspections:
- Thrust bearing inspection (measure clearances)
- Dry gas seal condition check
- Impeller clearance measurement
- Vibration baseline measurement
- Borescope inspection if available

### Return to Service Protocol
1. Complete all inspections
2. Restart at 50% load
3. Monitor vibration for 4 hours
4. Gradually increase to 100% if stable
5. Document all findings

### References: 49 CFR 192.615, API 618

abnormal_variants.description: Vibration remains at 0.7 in/s after 30 minutes in recycle
abnormal_variants.modified_expected_response: SUSTAINED HIGH VIBRATION - SHUTDOWN REQUIRED. Vibration above alarm for extended period indicates damage. Shutdown unit immediately. Do not return to service until bearing inspection completed. Potential thrust bearing failure.

abnormal_variants.description: Seal gas differential pressure drops during inspection
abnormal_variants.modified_expected_response: SEAL DAMAGE CONFIRMED. Dropping seal gas pressure indicates dry gas seal damage from surge. Unit cannot return to service until seals replaced. Major overhaul required. Order seals immediately.

name: Public Safety Protection - Third Party Strike During Repair
situation: During repair of previously identified coating damage, excavation crew strikes pipeline with backhoe bucket causing dent. Pipeline is 20-inch X52, MAOP 1,200 psig, operating pressure 1,000 psig. Dent depth measured at 0.45 inches (2.25% of diameter). Pipeline is near rural highway with moderate traffic (500 vehicles/day). Contractor is performing work under company supervision. No gas release detected. Repair was expected to take 3 hours, now extended. Dusk approaching in 2 hours. School bus route uses this highway with next pickup in 90 minutes.
task: Assess third-party damage per 192.614, determine if immediate repair required per 192.703, specify traffic control for continued operations, establish public safety exclusion, address contractor responsibility, and coordinate with school transportation.
expected_response_detailed:
## Third Party Damage Assessment per 192.614 and 192.703

### Damage Evaluation
Dent depth: 0.45 inches on 20-inch pipe = 2.25% of diameter
192.703 criteria: Dents >2% of OD require repair if stress concentrators present

### Immediate Actions
1. ASSESS for metal loss, cracks, or stress concentrators
2. Ultrasonic testing at dent location
3. If smooth dent <2%: May monitor
4. If >2% or stress concentrator: Immediate repair

### Repair Decision
0.45 inches = 2.25% > 2% threshold
REPAIR REQUIRED per 192.703
Options: Type B sleeve, hot tap fitting, or cut-out

### Public Safety Measures
- 75-foot minimum exclusion during repair
- Traffic control: Flaggers both directions
- Reduce speed limit to 25 mph
- School bus coordination: Delay pickup or alternate route

### Traffic Control for Night Operations
Additional lighting required
Flashing arrow boards
Pilot car if necessary
Notify county sheriff

### Contractor Responsibility
Report under 191.5 (third-party damage)
Repair costs: Contractor responsibility
Re-training required before future work

### References: 49 CFR 192.614, 192.703, 191.5

abnormal_variants.description: Gouge discovered at dent location with metal loss
abnormal_variants.modified_expected_response: CRITICAL DAMAGE - IMMEDIATE REPAIR. Gouge with metal loss requires immediate repair. May need pressure reduction. School bus route must be closed or diverted. Emergency repair crew dispatched.

abnormal_variants.description: Traffic accident occurs near worksite blocking access
abnormal_variants.modified_expected_response: ACCESS BLOCKED - EMERGENCY COORDINATION. Traffic accident blocks emergency access. Coordinate with law enforcement. Establish alternate access route. Consider temporary line shutdown if access compromised.

name: Hot Tapping on Sour Gas Pipeline
situation: A 16-inch sour gas gathering pipeline requires hot tapping to install a new well connection. Pipeline operates at 850 psig with H2S concentration of 1,200 ppm. Pipeline is API 5L X52N with 0.375-inch wall, installed in 2005. Nearest isolation valves are 8 miles upstream and 6 miles downstream. Gas volume between valves is approximately 45 MMCF. Ambient temperature is 40°F with 10 mph winds. Work location is 500 feet from nearest residence. All personnel are H2S trained and equipped with SCBA units.
task: Develop hot tap procedure for sour gas service per API 2201 and NACE MR0175. Specify material requirements for sour service, H2S monitoring protocols, emergency response procedures specific to H2S, evacuation zones, and personnel protection requirements. Calculate exclusion zones based on H2S concentration.
expected_response_detailed:
## Sour Gas Hot Tap Procedure per API 2201 and NACE MR0175

### Sour Service Classification
Per NACE MR0175/ISO 15156: H2S >4 ppm defines sour service
Current: 1,200 ppm H2S - SEVERE sour service

### Material Requirements per NACE MR0175
- Hot tap machine: Sour service rated materials (HRC <22)
- Fitting: NACE compliant, low hardness
- Welding: Low hydrogen electrodes, controlled heat input
- Gaskets: Sour service rated (spiral wound with graphite)

### H2S Monitoring Protocol
- Continuous H2S monitors at work site
- Personal monitors for all personnel (alarm at 10 ppm)
- Area monitors upwind and downwind
- SCBA on standby within 30 seconds

### Exclusion Zone Calculations
Base exclusion: 200 feet for sour gas hot tap
H2S adjustment: 1,200 ppm requires 400-foot radius
Residential at 500 feet: Outside exclusion, shelter in place

### Emergency Response
H2S alarm at 10 ppm: Don SCBA
H2S alarm at 20 ppm: Evacuate non-essential personnel
H2S alarm at 50 ppm: Full evacuation, abort hot tap

### Welding Requirements
Preheat: 200°F minimum per API 1104 for sour service
PWHT may be required depending on thickness
NACE compliant filler metals only

### References: API 2201, NACE MR0175/ISO 15156, API 1104

abnormal_variants.description: H2S monitor alarms at 35 ppm during welding
abnormal_variants.modified_expected_response: H2S RELEASE - ABORT HOT TAP. 35 ppm exceeds safe working limit. All personnel don SCBA. Evacuate non-essential personnel to 500 feet. Stop welding immediately. Investigate leak source.

abnormal_variants.description: SCBA unit fails for one worker
abnormal_variants.modified_expected_response: EQUIPMENT FAILURE - WORK STOPPAGE. Failed SCBA means worker cannot be in H2S environment. Remove worker from site immediately. No hot tap work without 100% SCBA availability. Replace or repair unit before continuing.

name: Confined Space Rescue - Unconscious Entrant
situation: Worker enters 10-foot deep manhole to inspect flow meter. Manhole is 6 feet diameter with ladder access. Worker was wearing 4-gas monitor and had SCBA on standby. After 8 minutes, worker radios that they feel dizzy. Radio goes silent. Attendant cannot see worker due to angle. Atmospheric readings at entrance: O2 19.8%, LEL 0%, H2S 0%, CO 0%. Attendant is trained but has never performed a rescue. Rescue equipment (tripod, winch, harness) is on site but not set up.
task: Execute confined space rescue per OSHA 1910.146(k). Specify rescue procedures, PPE requirements, communication protocols, and medical response. Address the decision of whether attendant should attempt rescue or call for help. Calculate rescue timing considerations.
expected_response_detailed:
## Confined Space Rescue per OSHA 1910.146(k)

### Immediate Actions (First 60 seconds)
1. CALL 911 immediately - report confined space emergency
2. DO NOT ENTER without SCBA - potential atmospheric hazard
3. Set up tripod and winch system
4. Summon additional help

### Rescue Decision
ATTENDANT MUST NOT ENTER ALONE per 1910.146(k)
Even with SCBA, solo rescue is prohibited
Wait for rescue team or additional personnel

### Rescue Setup (2-3 minutes)
- Deploy tripod over manhole
- Attach winch cable to rescue harness
- Don SCBA by rescuer
- Test communication

### Entry and Rescue (3-5 minutes)
- Rescuer descends with SCBA
- Assess victim condition
- Attach rescue harness to victim
- Victim lifted out using winch
- Rescuer exits

### Medical Response
- O2 administration immediately
- AED if no pulse
- Transport to hospital
- Suspect H2S or CO exposure despite readings

### Total Rescue Time
Target: <6 minutes from alarm to victim extrication

### References: OSHA 1910.146(k), API 2015

abnormal_variants.description: Entrant is hanging from ladder unconscious
abnormal_variants.modified_expected_response: SUSPENDED VICTIM - URGENT. Hanging victim has minutes before suspension trauma becomes fatal. Expedite rescue setup. Victim may need to be lowered to ground before extraction. Medical evaluation for suspension trauma required.

abnormal_variants.description: Rescuer's SCBA low air alarm activates during descent
abnormal_variants.modified_expected_response: LOW AIR - ABORT RESCUE. Rescuer must exit immediately. Send second rescuer. Never ignore low air alarm. Victim extraction delayed but rescuer safety priority.

name: Emergency Response - Pipeline Rupture in Waterway
situation: 24-inch natural gas transmission pipeline ruptures at river crossing. Pipeline is 15 feet below river bottom in HDD installation. Rupture creates crater in riverbed with visible bubbling and water disturbance. Pipeline operates at 1,100 psig, MAOP 1,440 psig. River is navigable waterway with recreational boat traffic. Downstream is drinking water intake for city of 50,000 people, 2 miles away. Upstream valve 12 miles away, downstream valve 18 miles away. Weather clear, light wind. Time is 10:00 AM on Saturday (peak recreation).
task: Execute emergency response per 49 CFR 192.615 and 192.617 for underwater rupture. Address waterway hazards, environmental protection, drinking water protection, isolation strategy, public safety for boaters, and regulatory notifications including Coast Guard and EPA.
expected_response_detailed:
## Underwater Pipeline Rupture Response

### Immediate Priorities
1. Public safety - boaters in area
2. Drinking water protection
3. Gas release control
4. Environmental assessment

### Isolation Strategy
Upstream valve: 12 miles - close immediately (4 minutes)
Downstream valve: 18 miles - close immediately (5 minutes)
Total isolation: 30 miles of pipe underwater

### Waterway Safety
- Contact Coast Guard immediately (VHF Ch 16)
- Establish 1,000-foot exclusion zone on water
- Notify local marine patrol
- Post warning buoys if safe to do so

### Drinking Water Protection
Notify water treatment plant immediately
Monitor intake for gas intrusion
Prepare to close intake if needed
Gas in water supply is explosion hazard

### Environmental Considerations
Natural gas primarily methane - limited water toxicity
But: Displaced sediment, habitat disturbance
Document for EPA notification

### Regulatory Notifications
- NRC: 1-800-424-8802
- Coast Guard: VHF Ch 16 or local sector
- EPA: Region office
- State environmental agency

### References: 49 CFR 192.615, 192.617, 33 CFR

abnormal_variants.description: Boaters observed fishing 200 feet from rupture
abnormal_variants.modified_expected_response: BOATERS IN DANGER - IMMEDIATE EVACUATION. Dispatch marine patrol to evacuate boaters. Risk of explosion from gas accumulation. Use loudhailers. Do not approach in motorized vessel due to ignition risk.

abnormal_variants.description: Drinking water intake shows gas detection
abnormal_variants.modified_expected_response: WATER SUPPLY CONTAMINATION - CLOSE INTAKE. Gas detected at intake requires immediate shutdown. City water supply compromised. Emergency water conservation. Alternative water sources required. Major incident escalation.

name: Excavation Near Pipeline - One Call Compliance
situation: Municipal water department plans to install new 12-inch water main parallel to existing 30-inch natural gas transmission pipeline. Water line will be 15 feet from gas pipeline centerline at closest approach. Excavation depth 8 feet, gas pipeline depth 4 feet. Water department called 811 and received marks. Pipeline operator locator marked gas line with white paint and flags. Water department wants to begin excavation Monday morning using trackhoe. No pre-excavation meeting held. Pipeline operates at 1,200 psig.
task: Review excavation plan per 49 CFR 192.614 and CGA Best Practices. Specify requirements before excavation begins, determine if hand digging is required, establish clearance requirements, specify monitoring during excavation, and address the lack of pre-excavation meeting.
expected_response_detailed:
## Excavation Planning per 192.614 and CGA Best Practices

### Pre-Excavation Requirements
1. Pre-excavation meeting REQUIRED (not held - MUST OCCUR)
2. Verify marks are still valid (811 tickets expire)
3. Confirm locate accuracy
4. Establish hand dig zone

### Hand Dig Requirements
Per CGA Best Practices:
- Hand dig or vacuum excavate within 24 inches of pipeline
- 15-foot separation means most work can be mechanized
- But: Final 24 inches near gas line must be hand dug

### Clearance Requirements
Minimum 12 inches vertical clearance
Horizontal clearance: As much as practicable
15 feet is acceptable separation

### Monitoring Requirements
- Pipeline operator representative on-site
- Continuous monitoring during excavation
- Stop work if pipeline exposed
- Daily safety briefings

### Missing Pre-Excavation Meeting
CRITICAL OMISSION
Must be held before work begins
Attendees: excavator, locator, pipeline operator, water department
Discuss: procedures, emergency contacts, line location

### References: 49 CFR 192.614, CGA Best Practices

abnormal_variants.description: Excavator hits unmarked fiber optic cable
abnormal_variants.modified_expected_response: UNMARKED FACILITY - STOP WORK. Unmarked cable strike indicates locate failure. All work stops until facility identified. Report to 811. Cable owner must locate. Potential 191.15 reportable incident.

abnormal_variants.description: Trackhoe bucket scrapes gas pipeline coating
abnormal_variants.modified_expected_response: PIPELINE CONTACT - DAMAGE ASSESSMENT. Any contact requires inspection. Stop excavation. Inspect for damage. May need UT or RT. Document per 192.614. Notify pipeline operator immediately.

name: Compressor Station Fire - Lube Oil System
situation: Fire detected in lube oil system of 10,000 HP reciprocating compressor. Fire started at bearing lube oil supply line rupture. Oil is spraying onto hot compressor surface. Fire detection system activated. Unit is running at 100% load. Compressor discharge temperature 280°F. Lube oil tank contains 500 gallons of turbine oil. Fire suppression system is CO2 type. Control room operator sees fire alarm and high temperature alarm. No personnel in compressor building.
task: Execute emergency response per API 2021 and NFPA 30. Determine ESD requirements, fire suppression strategy, personnel evacuation, and equipment protection. Address the risk of oil fire spread and potential for three-dimensional fire.
expected_response_detailed:
## Lube Oil Fire Response per API 2021

### Immediate Actions
1. ACTIVATE Unit ESD immediately
2. Discharge CO2 fire suppression
3. Isolate lube oil pump and close tank valves
4. Evacuate building

### Fire Characteristics
Spraying oil fire = THREE-DIMENSIONAL FIRE
Most dangerous type of liquid fire
Oil spray can reach 20+ feet
High risk of fire spread

### ESD Requirements
Full unit shutdown:
- Stop compressor
- Close suction and discharge valves
- Blowdown compressor
- Isolate lube oil system

### Fire Suppression
CO2 appropriate for oil fire
But: Three-dimensional fires difficult to extinguish
May require foam application
Water fog for cooling only (not extinguishment)

### Personnel Safety
- Full building evacuation
- 200-foot exclusion zone
- Accountability check
- No re-entry until fire out and atmosphere tested

### Post-Fire Actions
- Inspect for re-ignition
- Check lube oil tank level
- Assess compressor damage
- Document for investigation

### References: API 2021, NFPA 30, API 618

abnormal_variants.description: CO2 system fails to discharge
abnormal_variants.modified_expected_response: SUPPRESSION FAILURE - MANUAL FIREFIGHTING. CO2 failure requires manual response. Fire department with foam. Cool exposures with water. Do not use straight stream on oil fire. Consider letting controlled burn until oil depleted.

abnormal_variants.description: Fire spreads to gas piping creating gas/oil fire
abnormal_variants.modified_expected_response: GAS/OIL FIRE - STATION ESD. Gas fire addition requires full station shutdown. Evacuate to 500 feet. Defensive operations only. Do not extinguish gas fire. Cool exposures. Major incident escalation.

name: Sour Gas Well Blowout Response
situation: Sour gas well blowout occurs during workover operations. Well is flowing uncontrolled with estimated 2,000 ppm H2S in gas stream. Flow rate estimated 10 MMCFD. Wellhead pressure 3,500 psig. Well located in rural area with nearest residence 1 mile away. Wind 15 mph from west. 15 workers on location at time of blowout - 12 evacuated successfully, 3 missing. H2S monitors showing 50 ppm at 500 feet downwind. Well site has H2S contingency plan but no diverter or snubbing unit on site.
task: Execute well blowout response per API RP 53 and 192.615. Establish evacuation zones based on H2S dispersion, coordinate search for missing personnel, determine well control options, specify mutual aid requirements, and address environmental and public safety concerns.
expected_response_detailed:
## Sour Gas Blowout Response per API RP 53

### Immediate Priorities
1. ACCOUNT for all personnel - 3 missing
2. Establish safe perimeter
3. Protect public
4. Plan well control

### Evacuation Zones (H2S at 2,000 ppm)
IDLH Zone (>100 ppm): Estimated 1,000-foot radius
ERPG-2 Zone (30 ppm): Estimated 2-mile radius
Shelter in place: 3-mile radius

### Missing Personnel Search
CANNOT SEARCH until H2S below IDLH
Use helicopter with H2S monitor for recon
Rescue team with SCBA only
May need to wait for wind shift or flow reduction

### Well Control Options
1. Remote shut-in (if valves accessible)
2. Relief well (months)
3. Capping stack (days to weeks)
4. Vent and burn (if feasible)

### Mutual Aid
- Well control specialists (Boots & Coots, Wild Well)
- H2S response team
- Air ambulance on standby
- Additional SCBA units

### Public Protection
Evacuate residents within 2 miles
Road closures
Continuous air monitoring
Reverse 911 notification

### References: API RP 53, 49 CFR 192.615, API RP 55
expected_elements: Cannot search until H2S below IDLH

abnormal_variants.description: Wind shifts toward town of 5,000 people 5 miles away
abnormal_variants.modified_expected_response: WIND SHIFT - MAJOR EVACUATION. H2S cloud now threatening populated area. Evacuate town immediately. Request state emergency management. National Guard assistance. Media notification. Major incident command activation.

abnormal_variants.description: One missing person located unconscious 300 feet from well
abnormal_variants.modified_expected_response: RESCUE ATTEMPT - SCBA REQUIRED. Unconscious victim 300 feet from well in IDLH zone. Rescue team with SCBA only. Helicopter extraction if possible. Medical team standing by. Time critical - H2S exposure may be fatal.

name: Valve Failure - Emergency Isolation
situation: Upstream MOV on 42-inch transmission pipeline fails to close during routine test. Valve is critical isolation point for 50-mile segment serving metropolitan area. Pipeline operates at 1,200 psig, MAOP 1,440 psig. Valve has 36-inch bypass but no other isolation for 25 miles upstream. Valve was last exercised 6 months ago and operated normally. Current attempt was during monthly exercise. Motor actuator runs but valve does not move. Torque switch shows high torque alarm. Temperature 25°F.
task: Respond to valve failure per API RP 553. Determine alternative isolation methods, assess risk of continued operation, specify troubleshooting steps, determine if emergency repair is required, and address cold temperature effects on valve operation.
expected_response_detailed:
## Valve Failure Response per API RP 553

### Immediate Assessment
MOV runs but valve doesn't move = MECHANICAL BINDING
High torque alarm indicates obstruction or seizure
Cold weather (25°F) may be contributing factor

### Risk Assessment
No upstream isolation for 25 miles
If downstream event occurs, cannot isolate segment
METROPOLITAN AREA SERVICE - HIGH CONSEQUENCE

### Troubleshooting Steps
1. Check manual override (if accessible)
2. Attempt partial stroke in opposite direction
3. Apply external heat to valve body
4. Check for ice in actuator
5. Do not exceed torque limits

### Alternative Isolation
- 36-inch bypass valve (if operable)
- Upstream station ESD (25 miles away)
- Hot tap and line stop (days to arrange)

### Emergency Repair Decision
Valve MUST be repaired or replaced
Options:
1. Line stop and replace valve
2. Hot tap bypass and repair
3. Reduce pressure and attempt manual operation

### Cold Temperature Effects
25°F may cause:
- Lubricant thickening
- Seal adhesion
- Ice formation
Apply heat tracing or steam to warm valve

### References: API RP 553, API 6D

abnormal_variants.description: Bypass valve also fails to operate
abnormal_variants.modified_expected_response: DUAL VALVE FAILURE - CRITICAL. Both valves inoperable. Emergency condition. Reduce system pressure to minimum. Dispatch repair crew immediately. Consider line stop bypass. Metropolitan area service at risk.

abnormal_variants.description: Pipeline rupture occurs downstream while valve is failed
abnormal_variants.modified_expected_response: RUPTURE WITH FAILED ISOLATION - EMERGENCY. Cannot isolate rupture segment. Close next upstream valve (25 miles away). Evacuate large area. Major gas release. Full emergency response activation.

name: Abnormal Operations - Pipeline Overpressure Event
situation: Natural gas transmission pipeline experiences overpressure event. Pipeline MAOP is 1,440 psig. Pressure reached 1,520 psig (105% MAOP) for approximately 15 minutes before control system corrected. Event occurred when upstream compressor station failed to respond to shutdown signal. High pressure alarm activated at 1,450 psig. Overpressure protection system (PSV) did not open. Pipeline is Class 3 location with 45 dwellings per mile. No rupture or leak detected. Pressure now stabilized at 1,200 psig.
task: Respond to overpressure event per 49 CFR 192.615 and ASME B31.8. Assess potential damage, determine investigation requirements, specify PSV testing requirements, address control system failure, and document the incident for regulatory reporting.
expected_response_detailed:
## Overpressure Response per 192.615 and ASME B31.8

### Event Assessment
Pressure: 1,520 psig = 105.5% of MAOP (1,440 psig)
Duration: 15 minutes
ASME B31.8 allows up to 110% MAOP for short durations
But: PSV should have opened at 1,584 psig (110%)

### Potential Damage Assessment
15 minutes at 105% unlikely to cause immediate failure
But may have:
- Accelerated crack growth
- Coating damage at joints
- Increased stress on anomalies

### Immediate Actions
1. Inspect for leaks along pipeline
2. Verify pressure control restored
3. Test PSV immediately
4. Review compressor control system

### PSV Investigation
PSV did not open at 110% setpoint
Possible causes:
- Setpoint drift
- Blockage in sensing line
- Mechanical failure
PSV must be tested and repaired before return to service

### Control System Review
Compressor failed to respond to shutdown
Investigate:
- Signal failure
- Valve failure
- Control logic

### Documentation
Reportable per 191.15 if:
- Property damage >$50,000
- Investigation will determine

### References: 49 CFR 192.615, ASME B31.8, API RP 520

abnormal_variants.description: Leak discovered at flange during inspection
abnormal_variants.modified_expected_response: LEAK CONFIRMED - EMERGENCY REPAIR. Overpressure caused gasket failure. Immediate repair required. Reduce pressure or isolate segment. Incident reportable per 191.15. Root cause: overpressure event.

abnormal_variants.description: Pressure reached 1,600 psig (111% MAOP) for 5 minutes
abnormal_variants.modified_expected_response: SEVERE OVERPRESSURE - IMMEDIATE ACTION. 111% exceeds ASME B31.8 limits. Immediate integrity assessment required. PSV failure critical. Consider hydrostatic retest of segment. Major investigation required.

name: Public Safety - Pipeline Marker Inspection
situation: Routine aerial patrol identifies multiple pipeline marker deficiencies along 30-mile rural transmission line. Issues observed: 12 markers completely missing, 8 markers damaged/illegible, 5 markers knocked over but repairable, 3 markers obscured by vegetation. Pipeline is 24-inch, Class 2 location, MAOP 1,200 psig. Right-of-way crosses 6 roads and 2 railroads. Recent construction activity noted in area with missing markers. Pipeline company has 45-day marker inspection interval per procedure.
task: Assess marker deficiencies per 49 CFR 192.707 and API RP 1160. Prioritize repairs, specify temporary measures, determine if excavation monitoring is required, address the construction activity correlation, and establish corrective action timeline.
expected_response_detailed:
## Pipeline Marker Assessment per 192.707

### Regulatory Requirements
192.707 requires markers at:
- Road crossings
- Railroad crossings
- Water crossings
- At intervals along line

### Priority Classification
CRITICAL (immediate repair):
- Missing markers at road/railroad crossings
- Any crossing without visible marker

HIGH (within 7 days):
- Missing markers in high consequence areas
- Illegible crossing markers

MEDIUM (within 30 days):
- Missing interval markers
- Damaged but visible markers

### Temporary Measures
- Temporary stakes with flags at critical crossings
- Increased patrol frequency
- Notify One Call center of area

### Construction Activity Correlation
Missing markers near construction = HIGH RISK
Dispatch inspector to construction sites immediately
Verify no unauthorized excavation

### Corrective Action Timeline
Critical: 24 hours
High: 7 days
Medium: 30 days
All repairs: 45 days

### References: 49 CFR 192.707, API RP 1160

abnormal_variants.description: Excavation observed near missing marker location
abnormal_variants.modified_expected_response: UNAUTHORIZED EXCAVATION - IMMEDIATE RESPONSE. Excavation near missing marker is emergency. Stop work immediately. Verify no pipeline contact. Law enforcement notification. Damage assessment required.

abnormal_variants.description: Railroad crossing marker missing for 60 days per records
abnormal_variants.modified_expected_response: LONG-TERM DEFICIENCY - REGULATORY VIOLATION. 60-day missing marker at railroad is serious violation. Immediate temporary marking. PHMSA notification may be required. Corrective action plan required.

name: Operator Qualification Testing - Welder Performance
situation: Pipeline company is conducting operator qualification testing for welders performing repair work on live gas pipelines. Test scenario: Welders must demonstrate ability to weld a 12-inch branch connection on pressurized pipeline (simulated). Test conditions: API 1104 requirements, X52 material, 0.375-inch wall, E6010 root/E7018 fill. Two welders being tested: Welder A has 20 years experience but failed visual inspection on last two production welds. Welder B has 3 years experience with excellent production record. Both passed written exam. Practical test shows: Welder A - acceptable root pass but excessive undercut on cap. Welder B - good root pass, acceptable cap, proper profile.
task: Evaluate welder qualification per 49 CFR 192 Subpart N and API 1104. Determine pass/fail for each welder, specify requalification requirements, address Welder A's recent performance issues, and establish ongoing monitoring requirements.
expected_response_detailed:
## Welder Qualification per 192 Subpart N and API 1104

### API 1104 Acceptance Criteria
Visual inspection per Section 9:
- Undercut: Maximum 1/32 inch (0.031 in)
- Reinforcement: Maximum 1/8 inch
- Profile: Smooth transition to base metal

### Welder A Evaluation
Excessive undercut on cap
If undercut >1/32 inch: REJECT
Recent production failures + test failure = PERFORMANCE ISSUE

### Welder B Evaluation
Good root pass
Acceptable cap
Proper profile
RESULT: PASS

### Welder A Disposition
FAILED qualification test
Cannot perform covered tasks until requalified
Required actions:
1. Additional training on cap welding
2. Re-test after 30 days minimum
3. Review recent production weld failures
4. Possible welder qualification suspension

### Ongoing Monitoring
Per 192.807: Requalification every 5 years
But: Production failures require immediate review
Welders with failures: Annual requalification recommended

### Documentation
Maintain records per 192.809
Test results, deficiencies, corrective actions
Retention: Employment + 5 years

### References: 49 CFR 192.805, 192.807, 192.809, API 1104

abnormal_variants.description: Radiographic testing reveals lack of fusion in Welder B's root pass
abnormal_variants.modified_expected_response: RT FAILURE - BOTH WELDERS FAIL. Lack of fusion is rejectable defect per API 1104. Both welders fail qualification. Stop testing. Review welding procedure. Re-train both welders. Re-test after 30 days.

abnormal_variants.description: Welder A refuses additional training citing seniority
abnormal_variants.modified_expected_response: QUALIFICATION MANDATORY - NO EXCEPTIONS. Seniority does not override qualification requirements. Welder A cannot perform covered tasks until passed requalification. Union grievance does not affect safety requirements. Document refusal.

name: Pipeline Rupture Emergency Response with Ignition
situation: 16-inch natural gas transmission pipeline ruptures with ignition in residential area. Operating pressure was 1,100 psig. Rupture occurred near elementary school. Fire visible 300 feet high. Multiple structures burning. 911 calls flooding in. On-call crew is 45 minutes away. Local fire department on scene but requesting guidance. Gas flow continues unabated. School evacuation in progress but incomplete.
task: Direct immediate emergency response per 49 CFR 192.615. Establish evacuation zones, coordinate with fire department, manage public safety, implement pipeline isolation.
expected_response_summary: Immediate evacuation establishment, coordination with fire department on controlled burn strategy, pipeline isolation sequence, public safety measures per API 2021 and NIMS.
expected_response_detailed:
## Pipeline Rupture Emergency Response - Immediate Actions per 49 CFR 192.615

### Primary Life Safety Actions (0-5 minutes)
1. **ESTABLISH EVACUATION ZONES IMMEDIATELY**
   - Hot Zone: 500-foot radius from rupture - NO ENTRY
   - Warm Zone: 500-2,000 feet downwind - authorized personnel only
   - Cold Zone: Beyond 2,000 feet or upwind - command positions
   - School MUST complete evacuation NOW (adds 250 students to evacuation)

2. **CRITICAL DECISION - DO NOT EXTINGUISH BURNING GAS**
   Per API 2021: Burning released gas is SAFER than accumulated unignited gas
   - Direct fire department to DEFENSIVE operations only
   - Cool exposed structures with water - do NOT direct at flame
   - Allow controlled burn until complete isolation
   - **Extinguishment before isolation = vapor cloud explosion risk**

3. **IMMEDIATE PIPELINE ISOLATION**
   - Contact Control Room for MOV closure
   - Upstream and downstream valves within 15 minutes target
   - Gas will continue burning 30-60 minutes post-isolation

### Fire Department Coordination
- Establish Unified Command per NIMS
- Provide technical advisor on pipeline operations
- Share gas composition data if requested
- Coordinate water supply for cooling operations

### School Coordination
- Verify 100% student evacuation account
- Establish reunification point upwind
- Account for all staff and students
- Communicate with parents via school district

### Public Notifications
- Media statement through PIO
- Shelter locations for displaced residents
- Estimated timeline for gas burnout
- No re-entry until safety cleared

### Technical Considerations
- 16-inch × 1,100 psig = significant release rate
- Thermal radiation at 300 ft: ~8,000 BTU/hr-ft²
- Burn time estimate: 45-90 minutes

**REFERENCES**: 49 CFR 192.615, API 2021, NIMS ICS-100

abnormal_variants.description: Fire department attempts to extinguish gas fire
abnormal_variants.modified_expected_response: IMMEDIATE INTERVENTION REQUIRED - DO NOT EXTINGUISH. Notify fire chief that extinguishing burning gas before isolation creates explosive vapor cloud. Order immediate cessation of extinguishment. Reinforce defensive operations only. If vapor cloud forms, expand evacuation to 2,000 feet immediately.

abnormal_variants.description: Upstream MOV fails to close remotely
abnormal_variants.modified_expected_response: MANUAL VALVE CLOSURE REQUIRED. Dispatch valve technician team with PPE and SCBA to manually close upstream valve. Fire department escort if safe. Extended burn duration expected. Expand evacuation if needed.

name: H2S Monitoring Protocol
expected_response_detailed: Sour gas operations with potential H2S require: continuous 4-gas monitoring (H2S, LEL, O2, CO), personal monitors for each worker, alarm at 10 ppm H2S (IDLH 100 ppm), wind direction indicators, upwind positioning for equipment, up to 300 ppm requires supplied air, above 300 ppm requires SCBA, buddy system mandatory, site security to prevent exposure of unauthorized personnel per API RP 49.

name: Gas Migration During Excavation
expected_response_detailed: Excavation near 20-inch line with soil gas odor requires: immediate evacuation to 300-foot radius, no ignition sources within 500 feet, continuous LEL monitoring every 5 minutes, call 911 and fire department, vapor barriers if migration toward structures, forced ventilation if entering work area, PRCS classification if entering excavation, atmospheric monitoring top and bottom of excavation, rescue tripod if confined space entry required per OSHA 1926.

name: Crane Operation Near Overhead Lines
expected_response_detailed: Crane near 345kV lines requires: 15-foot minimum clearance (16 for insulated lines), spotter with radio required, crane grounded/boom bonded to ground, insulated links if working within minimums (requires de-energization or cover-up), load chart adjusted for proximity, power company notification 48 hours in advance, designated signaller required, barricades at 10-foot radius, arc flash PPE 8+ cal/cm², emergency descent plan if contact occurs per OSHA 1926.1408.

name: Lock Out Tag Out During Valve Maintenance
expected_response_detailed: Valve maintenance requiring LOTO per 192.805: isolate all energy sources (upstream/downstream manual valves), lock valves in closed position (locks on valve stems), tag valves with warning tags, verify zero energy state (confirm depressurized, drain if possible), stored energy dissipation (bleed trapped pressure), affected operator notification, LOTO documentation, individual locks for each worker (not group locks), 24-hour rule for shift changes, testing before work begins per OSHA 1910.147.

name: Flame Arrestor Installation Verification
expected_response_detailed: Flame arrestor at tank vent requires: correct element for gas group (D for natural gas, C for sour), bidirectional or unidirectional based on layout, pressure drop verification <0.1 psi at max flow, element cleaning annually or when pressure drop increases 50%, temperature rating >operating, pipe length within manufacturer specs, inspection window required, documentation including model and date, bypass prohibited, explosion-proof rating for electrical nearby per API 2028.

name: Personal Fall Arrest System Inspection
expected_response_detailed: PFAS required above 4 feet per 1926.501: full body harness with dorsal D-ring, 6-foot shock-absorbing lanyard or SRL, anchor rated 5,000 lbs or 2× max arrest force, inspection before each use (webbing, stitching, hardware), storage away from chemicals/UV, 6-month formal inspection, removal from service if damaged, never tie off at waist level, positioning devices separate from fall arrest, rescue plan required per OSHA 1926.502.

name: Fire Watch During Shutdown Maintenance
expected_response_detailed: Fire watch during station turnaround requires: dedicated person (no other duties), continuous visual monitoring, appropriate fire extinguisher (Class B for gas, Class C for electrical), immediate 911 access, 30-60 minute post-work watch after hot work, perimeter sweep every 15 minutes, communication radio with control room, authority to stop work, trained in extinguisher use, documentation of watch start/end times per API 2219 Section 4.6.

name: Chemical Exposure Response
expected_response_detailed: Glycol exposure to face requires: immediate flushing with tepid water for 15 minutes minimum, remove contact lenses if present, call poison control 1-800-222-1222, medical evaluation even if no immediate symptoms, SDS review for methanol content if present, buddy system for observation, documentation of exposure time and concentration, review PPE effectiveness and training gap, replace contaminated clothing, no topical ointments without medical direction per ANSI Z358.1.

name: Atmospheric Testing in Confined Space
expected_response_detailed: PRCS entry requires: O2 19.5-23.5% (alarm if outside), LEL <10% (entry prohibited if >20%), H2S <10 ppm, CO <25 ppm (ceiling), SO2 <2 ppm, benzene <0.5 ppm (8-hour), test at top middle bottom (gases stratify by density), continuous monitoring with alarm, pump-style for remote sampling, calibration within 30 days per OSHA 1910.146.

name: Grounding During Transfer Operations
expected_response_detailed: Liquid transfer to tank truck requires: bonding cable connected before dome opening, resistance <10 ohms between vessel and truck, static grounding clamp verified (not just parked truck), no transfer if thunderstorm within 5 miles, vapor recovery connection if required by state, bonding maintained until dome closed, no loose clothing that could generate static, conductive footwear for workers, documentation of grounding verification per NFPA 77.

name: Hydrogen Sulfide Release Response
expected_response_detailed: H2S release at 75 ppm requires: immediate evacuation upwind and upgrade (H2S heavier than air), SCBA for rescue only (not entry), continuous wind monitoring, headcount at muster, 911 notification, downwind shelter-in-place to 0.5 mile, lockout of source if accessible, no mouth-to-mouth, buddy-breathing prohibited, emergency showers for exposure, medical evaluation for all exposed even asymptomatic per API RP 55.

name: Emergency Isolation System Failure
expected_response_detailed: ESD valve fails to close: attempt manual override (local handwheel), if hydraulic failure use backup nitrogen accumulator, if actuator seized isolate pneumatic supply and manual gear operator, manual gate valve isolation upstream/downstream, coordinate with control for depressurization block, temporary blind if needed, no hot work during manual isolation, document failure per PHMSA incident if safety-related equipment, expedited repair of safety system.

name: Dropped Object Prevention
expected_response_detailed: Tools at height require: wrist lanyard or tool tether (rated 2× tool weight), hard hat chin strap (not just suspension), toe boards 4-inch minimum on platforms, netting under overhead work, exclusion zone below lifting area (radius = height), dropped object calculator for trajectory, tool inventory before and after, red zone exclusion for personnel, crane tag lines controlled from ground, inspection of lifting gear before each lift per OSHA 1926.

name: Respiratory Protection Selection
expected_response_detailed: APR vs supplied air: APR for IDLH if 1/4 IDLH or less with cartridge, full-face APR mandatory for >TLV, P100 filter for particulates, organic vapor for solvents, breakthrough detection required, supplied air for IDLH (>H2S or low O2), SAR duration calculation (30-60 min typical), SCBA for escape only or entry, fit testing annual (qualitative for half-mask, quantitative for full-face), facial hair seal prohibition per OSHA 1910.134.

name: Safe Work Permit Closure
expected_response_detailed: Work completion requires: affected area inspected for tools/materials removal, equipment returned to service (valves reopened), blinds removed ( accounted for), hot work area 30-60 minute post-fire watch completed, LOTO devices removed by authorized persons, personnel cleared from area, handover to operations, documentation closed and retained 1 year minimum, lessons learned documented, incident report if applicable.

name: Hydraulic System Pressurization
expected_response_detailed: Hydraulic press to 5,000 psi requires: pressure relief valve rated test and tagged annually, accumulator pressure check, whip sock or restraints on hoses <500 psi rating, shielding for potential failure direction, PPE (safety glasses face shield), pressure gage calibration current, no quick-connect under pressure, bleed before disconnect, rated fittings only (no homemade adapters), documentation of test pressure and duration per ASME B31.3.

name: Trench Shoring Design Load
expected_response_detailed: 8-foot excavation in Type C soil: trench box required (sloping not practical in built-up area), soil load calculation: 120 pcf × depth = 960 psf lateral, surcharge for equipment traffic add 250 psf minimum, tabulated data from manufacturer required, daily inspection before entry, water removal if present (dewatering system), no work in excavation during rain, ladder within 25 feet horizontal, shoring extends 18 inches above grade, top rail 42 inches per OSHA 1926 Subpart P.

name: Electrical Safety Approach Limits
expected_response_detailed: 70E approach distances: limited approach boundary for 480V = 42 inches, restricted (qualified only) = 12 inches, qualified means trained and PPE, arc flash boundary calculated incident energy <1.2 cal/cm² (no PPE), energized work permit required within limited boundary, insulated tools minimum rating, arc-rated clothing 8 cal/cm² minimum for 480V panel work per NFPA 70E Table 130.7(C)(15)(a).

name: Emergency Shutdown Activation
expected_response_detailed: ESD button activation: immediate trip of assigned equipment, no override or delay (fail-safe design), notification to control center within 30 seconds, field verification of valve closure, isolation verification via pressure, investigation of cause before restart, 24-hour hold on operations if safety system triggered, incident investigation per PHMSA, corrective action before return to service, documentation of incident per API RP 1165.

name: Asbestos Sampling Protocol
expected_response_detailed: Suspected ACM requires: NIOSH 7400 or PLM sampling (bulk), 9 square feet minimum sample, certified industrial hygienist for sampling, wet methods for disturbance, HEPA vacuums, negative pressure enclosure if removal required, personal air monitoring during work, clearance sampling after work, disposal at licensed facility per EPA NESHAP, 40 CFR 61, written notification to state if >160 square feet removal.

name: Pressure Testing Personnel Safety
expected_response_detailed: Hydrostatic test at 1.5× MAOP requires: pressure gage in visible location to operator, barricaded area 100-foot radius minimum (test medium energy calculation if larger), no personnel in barricaded zone during pump-on hold period, dual isolation valves when possible, slow pressurization rate <100 psi/minute, monitored hold period 8 hours typically, pressure chart recording documentation, depressurize before personnel entry for inspection, test limits marked on gage.

name: Siloed Work Zone Notification
expected_response_detailed: SIMOPS (simultaneous operations) requires: coordination meeting before work, JSA for interface hazards, work area identification on map, radio communication between crews, exclusion zone 50-foot minimum, schedule coordination to prevent simultaneous energization, common permit holder if adjacent, emergency response unified, communication plan documented, stop-work authority between groups per company procedures.

name: Temporary Heating Equipment
expected_response_detailed: Indirect fired heaters for pipeline warming: minimum 25-foot from combustibles, 10-foot from structures with 1-hour rated barrier, ventilation 6 air changes, CO monitoring at 25 ppm, LEL monitoring continuous, fuel line protection from damage, drip leg on fuel supply, emergency shutoff at entry, ignition source control, equipment rated for hazardous location if within classified area per NFPA 30A and NFPA 54.

name: Explosive Atmosphere Classification
expected_response_detailed: NFPA 70 Class I Div 1 vs Div 2: Div 1 - gases expected normally, Div 2 - gases only under abnormal conditions. Zone 0 continuous, Zone 1 intermittent, Zone 2 abnormal. Sources: compressor seals (Zone 1), valves (Zone 2), open drains (Zone 1). Extent: 15-foot horizontal typically, 3-5 feet vertical above potential source. Purging to reduce classification: 6 air changes before equipment startup per NFPA 496. Documentation on area classification drawings.

name: Hand Signal Communication
expected_response_detailed: Crane hand signals per ASME B30.5: stop (arm extended horizontal, forearm vertical), boom up/down (thumbs up/fist), lower/load (point down with hand), swing (point to direction), emergency stop (cross arms above head). Only one signaller at a time. Hand or radio only (no voice alone). Whistle 1 beep stop, 2 beep boom up, 3 beep boom down, continuous emergency. Signal visual confirmation required.

name: Overpressure Protection Bypass
expected_response_detailed: Relief valve maintenance: management of change process, MOC documentation, temporary protection via second relief valve or pressure control, operating pressure reduction to 80% of relief set, continuous operator presence, 24-hour maximum bypass duration, pressure recorder documenting safety, fire watch equivalent for overpressure, return-to-service checklist, documentation of temporary configuration, operations and maintenance notification per API RP 520 and company procedure.

name: Dust Hazard Analysis
expected_response_detailed: Combustible dust per NFPA 654: material testing MIE and MEC, housekeeping to prevent accumulation >1/32 inch depth, deflagration venting if enclosed, electrical classification for dust (Class II Groups E F G), bonding and grounding for static, dust collection systems explosion protected, building egress requirements, employee training on dust hazards, hot work permit in dust areas, ventilation requirements.

name: Portable Fire Extinguisher Placement
expected_response_detailed: NFPA 10: maximum 75-foot travel distance for Class A, 50-foot for Class B hazards, 30-foot for Class C (electrical), 30-foot for Class D (metals). Mount height: top 3.5-5 feet, bottom 4 inches minimum clearance. Inspection monthly (tag), maintenance annually, hydrostatic test per schedule (5 years for water, 12 for CO2). Type selection: ABC for general, BC for energized electrical, D for specific metals. Size 10 lb minimum for industrial.

name: Confined Space Rescue Planning
expected_response_detailed: OSHA 1910.146 rescue: on-site team (12+ members, trained annually) or off-site (response time <5 minutes, notification verified). Equipment: tripod/winch, SCBAs, first aid, AED, radios. Non-entry rescue preferred (retrieval equipment attached to entrant). Plan rehearsal annually, documentation of drill scenarios. Team training: PPE use, victim packaging, atmospheric monitoring, medical evaluation procedures. Coordination with 911 if external rescue used.

name: Chemical Storage Incompatibility
expected_response_detailed: Secondary containment: 110% of largest container or 10% of total volume. Separation of incompatible materials: acids away from caustics, oxidizers away from flammables, water reactives in dry areas. Ventilation for volatile chemicals, explosion-proof electrical for flammables, fire suppression appropriate for chemical class, spill kit accessible, SDS posted, inventory, NFPA 704 placarding on containers per OSHA 1910.1200 HazCom.

name: Emergency Evacuation Assembly Point
expected_response_detailed: Assembly point selection: upwind and upgrade from gas sources, 500-foot minimum from facility, headcount muster visible to emergency responders, multiple assembly points for large sites (prevents over-crowding), accessibility for emergency vehicles, sign posting, lighting for night evacuation, accountability procedures (checklist), all-clear signal, no re-entry until authorized by emergency commander per emergency action plan.

name: Process Safety Information Update
expected_response_detailed: PSI per OSHA PSM: chemical hazards, technology, equipment. Update triggers: process changes (MOC), equipment replacement (like-for-like vs modification), new hazards identified, PHA findings, incident investigations, vendor notifications. Documentation: revision date, reason for change, affected documents. Distribution: affected personnel, contractors, operations, maintenance. Management of Change process for modifications. Retention: life of process plus 10 years typically per 29 CFR 1910.119.

name: Isolation Procedure Dual Block
expected_response_detailed: Double block and bleed per OSHA 1910.147: two valves between hazardous energy and work zone, bleed valve between them to vent. Order: close block 1, close block 2, open bleed, verify zero pressure, LOTO all three. Alternative: spectacle blind, spade, blind flange. For fluid systems: drain between blocks. For gas: vent to safe location or flare. Inspection before installation, torque to specification, pressure test leak detection.

name: Safety Shower Maintenance
expected_response_detailed: ANSI Z358.1: activate weekly (maintain equipment, flush lines), inspect monthly (flow rate, temperature), test annually (15-minute operation). Specifications: 20 gpm flow minimum, tepid water 60-100°F, 96 inches stream height, 20-inch diameter coverage, 10-second activation reach, stay-open valve, self-draining, signage high visibility (green on white), plumbed not portable (unless temporary), no doors to pass through, lighting adequate.

name: Oxygen Enriched Atmosphere
expected_response_detailed: O2 >23.5% is oxygen enriched (IDLH >60%). Hazards: extreme fire hazard (normally non-flammables burn), hyperoxia. Sources: improperly stored O2 cylinders, medical oxygen, welding O2 leaks. Monitoring: O2 content continuously if enrichment possible. Response: ventilate area, remove ignition sources, respiratory protection if O2 >60%, investigate source, air-line respirator (not SCBA - hyperoxia risk). Storage: oxygen separated from flammables by 20 feet or 1-hour rated barrier.

name: Scaffold Competent Person
expected_response_detailed: OSHA 1926 Subpart L: competent person qualified to identify hazards, authority to stop work, conduct inspections daily and after incidents, supervise erection/dismantling. Requirements: maximum planking 12 inches overhang, guardrails 42 inches high, midrails, Toeboards 4 inches, access ladder every 30 feet horizontal, base plates on stable ground, 100% planking on platforms, load capacity 4× intended load. Inspection before each shift and after weather event.

name: Emergency Response Mutual Aid
expected_response_detailed: Mutual aid agreement: response resources sharing (equipment, personnel), geographic coverage, activation procedures, communication protocols, training coordination, cost sharing, liability clarification, regular exercises, 24/7 contact information, equipment inventory update. Legal review required. Response time commitment. NIMS compliance if interfacing with government response. Documentation review annually. Activation authority defined.

name: Diesel Engine Air Intake Shutdown
expected_response_detailed: Diesel runaway danger: ingestion of hydrocarbon vapor causes uncontrollable speed increase, explosion hazard. Air intake shutdown valves: automatic activation on gas detection (fixed), manual pull-cable for portable equipment, butterfly or guillotine design, fail-safe spring closure. Testing: monthly manual activation, annual automatic test, lubrication maintenance, documentation. Clear labeling. Location accessible, signage. Runaway prevention alternatives: manual fuel cutoff (not reliable), overspeed trip, air supply closure only effective method.

name: Lifting Plan Critical Pick
expected_response_detailed: Critical lift per ASME B30 (multi-crane, personnel, near energized lines, >75% rated capacity, load path over occupied buildings): engineered lift plan, calculations for rigging, center of gravity determination, multiple crane coordination, 10-foot minimum clear of powerlines, signal person per crane, tag lines on load, operator verification of capacity, weather restrictions (wind >25 mph, thunderstorm), pre-lift meeting with all personnel, two-week advance notice, engineering review of crane mats, soil bearing capacity.

name: First Aid Kit Requirements
expected_response_detailed: OSHA 1910.151(b): medical supplies readily accessible, trained personnel in absence of infirmary. ANSI Z308.1 minimum contents: absorbent compress, adhesive bandages, adhesive tape, antibiotic treatment, antiseptic, breathing barrier, burn dressing, cold pack, eye covering, eye wash, sterile pads, trauma pads, gloves, scissors, instruction booklet. Unit-type A (low risk 2-25 employees) or B (high risk/modular). Inspection monthly. Expiration date check for medicines/peroxide.

name: Safety Culture Assessment
expected_response_detailed: Safety culture indicators: near miss reporting rate (leading), incident investigation quality, management engagement (walkthroughs), employee participation in JSA, stop work authority usage (encouraged not punished), learning from incidents, pre-startup review thoroughness, procedure compliance audits, safety meeting attendance, perception surveys. Assessment: interviews at all levels, behavior observation, document review, incident trend analysis. Recommendations prioritized by risk. Follow-up metrics established per API RP 754 process safety indicators.

name: Hot Work Near Combustibles
expected_response_detailed: Hot work clearance: 35-foot minimum from combustibles if no shielding, or fire-resistant shields (welding blankets 1-hour rated) within 15-foot, fire watch 100% of time. Sparks travel distance: 35-foot minimum fire watch zone, extensions for elevation. Shielding: fire blankets, metal screens, shields. Flammable clearance: all combustibles removed or covered. Ventilation: no forced air disturbing sparks toward combustibles. Fire watch: 30-60 minutes post-work. Documentation: clearance inspection signed off.

name: Mobile Crane Setup
expected_response_detailed: Outrigger mat sizing: 3× outrigger float area, distribute load over soil bearing capacity (3,000 psf typical). Mat check: no gaps, centered under float, level setup. Stability: 360-degree level indicator, outriggers fully extended or per chart. Ground bearing pressure calculation: total crane weight outrigger area vs soil bearing. Geotechnical input if questionable soil. Wind: reduce charts above 20 mph, no lifts above 35 mph.

name: Noise Exposure Controls
expected_response_detailed: OSHA 1926.52: 90 dBA 8-hour permissible exposure, 85 dBA action level (hearing conservation). Engineering controls: enclosures, barriers, equipment maintenance reducing noise. Administrative: job rotation, limiting exposure time. PPE: hearing protection (NRR 25+) if >85 dBA. Audiometric testing annually for exposed. Signage: hearing protection required >85 dBA areas. Measurement: sound level meter 3-foot from ear. High noise equipment: compressor, pumps, venting.

name: Hot Tapping Safety
expected_response_detailed: Hot tap procedure: hot tap machine on isolated fitting, equalize pressure, retract cutter, install valve, remove machine. Safety: no welding on hollow walls below 50°F, nitrogen purging for all hot taps, line stop for high risk, fire watch continuous, hot work permit, isolation distance maintained. Maximum pressure for hot tap: typically 80% of fitting rating. Cutter retention: positive retention required, never drop pieces in line.

name: Vehicle Spotter Requirements
expected_response_detailed: Spotter for backing vehicles: established visual contact, standard hand signals (point direction), radio backup communication, spotter never between vehicle and fixed object, operator must stop if spotter not visible, spotter has authority to stop, dedicated role (no other duties), high-visibility PPE, daylight or adequate lighting, spotter position out of path but visible.

name: Emergency Shutdown Bypass
expected_response_detailed: ESD bypass for testing: MOC required, management authorization, temporary condition <24 hours, operator present, manual ESD pushbutton functional, documentation, fire watch or equivalent for period. Not for: convenience, normal operation, maintenance if alternative protection. Restoration: immediate. If bypass needed >24 hours: approved MOC, risk assessment, compensatory measures detailed. Testing: annual trip test, partial stroke test quarterly.

name: Chemical Compatibility Storage
expected_response_detailed: Incompatibility groups: acids (separated from bases), oxidizers (separated from organics), flammables (separated from oxidizers), water-reactive (dry area), toxics (ventilated). Storage separation: distance or fire-rated wall. Secondary containment: 110% largest container. Ventilation: 6 air changes gas areas. Signs: NFPA 704 placards. SDS: accessible. Fire suppression: appropriate for chemical class.

name: Pipeline Pigging Safety
expected_response_detailed: Pig launching: communication with control center, valves lined up before opening closure, equalize pressure before traps, never force pig past sticking point (depression could occur), launcher barrel purged before opening, pig tracking confirmation, receiver pressured before opening. Velocity: maintain 2-5 ft/sec to prevent stuck. Bypass: around station if no piggable valves. Venting: to safe location or flare.

name: Temporary Structures Wind Limits
expected_response_detailed: Wind limits: tents/scaffolds remove at 25 mph sustained, cranes cease operations at 20-35 mph depending on load surface (chart used), personnel lifts 25 mph max, personnel on high wind >35 mph cease, lightning: <6 miles distance cease immediately. Monitoring: weather station, visual flags, anemometer. Tiedown: temporary structures secured or lowered.

name: Confined Space Ventilation
expected_response_detailed: Forced air ventilation: blower at fresh air source ducted to space, explosion-proof electrical if gas present, minimum 6 air changes/hour, continuous during entry, ventilation effectiveness verified by air monitoring (O2, LEL), not a substitute for atmosphere testing or PPE. Exhaust should ventilate layers, ducts positioned at bottom for low vapor, top for removal. Temporary ventilation documented in permit.
